steps.title: Identify the key example
steps.content: The dash introduces a marketing campaign as the concrete example of what “can play a big role” in a book’s commercial success. A marketing campaign is fundamentally a publicity effort, so the blank must name the category that marketing campaigns belong to.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'originality': means being novel or creative — a quality of the book itself, not something a marketing campaign provides. ✗
  • Choice B — 'publicity': means public promotion and attention — exactly what a well-executed marketing campaign generates. ✓
  • Choice C — 'complexity': means having many intricate parts — a property of the book’s content, not of a marketing campaign. ✗
  • Choice D — 'practicality': means usefulness for everyday purposes — unrelated to what a marketing campaign does. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
